### handling if statements {.smaller}

```
if (a > 0) {
    ...
}
```

<hr>

*  solve $a > 0$ and $a \le 0$ cases separately
*  form tree of possible paths
   *  (issue for later: how many???)
*  track constraints, use when finding solutions

### example 1  {.smaller}

::::: {.columns}

:::: {.column width=300px}

```c
void foo(int a, int b) {
  /* (0) */
  if (a != 0) {
    b -= 2;
    a += b;
  }
  /* (1) */
  if (b < 5) {
    b += 4;
  }
  /* (2) */
  assert(a + b != 5);
}
```

::::

:::: {.column width=650px}

<!-- FIXME: copy of the text on the figure some where -->

::: {.r-stack}

![](/symbolic/texfig/choosePath1.figure-1.svg){width=800px .fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=1 fig-alt="at (0), a = alpha, b = beta; alpha and beta will represent, as before, our initial arguments."}

![](/symbolic/texfig/choosePath1.figure-2.svg){width=800px .fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=2 fig-alt="from (0) we branch into multiple states based on a not equal 0, forming a tree. For now, we'll just consider one path down to a leaf of the tree."}

![](/symbolic/texfig/choosePath1.figure-3.svg){width=800px .fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=3 fig-alt="if a not equal 0 is false, then at (1) we have a state alpha = 0, a = alpha, b = beta"}

![](/symbolic/texfig/choosePath1.figure-4.svg){width=800px .fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=4 fig-alt="from that state we further have to branch on b < 5"}

![](/symbolic/texfig/choosePath1.figure-5.svg){width=800px .fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=5 fig-alt="when b < 5 is false, then at (2) we have a state alpha = 0, beta >= 5; where a = alpha, b = beta"}

![](/symbolic/texfig/choosePath1.figure-6.svg){width=800px .fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=6 fig-alt="we can take that last state and figure out how to fail the assertion. We need the conditions from the state (alpha = 0, beta >= 5) plus a + b = 5, which translates to alpha + beta = 5. This has no solution."}

![](/symbolic/texfig/choosePath1.figure-7.svg){width=800px .fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=7 fig-alt="we also need to consider the other cases for the if statements. So if we b < 5 was true (but a != 0 false) we have an additional state at  (2) with beta < 5 (instead of (beta >= 5) and b = beta + 4 (instead of beta). Solve those equations (alpha=0, beta<5, a+b=5 or (after substitution) alpha + beta + 4 = 5) we get for that yield a solution of alpha = 0, beta = 1."}

![](/symbolic/texfig/choosePath1.figure-8.svg){width=800px .fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=8 fig-alt="doing the same for the other side, we find more solutions, for the states resulkting from assuming a != 0 is true instead of false."}

:::
 
::::

:::::

 [Adapted from Hicks, ‘‘Symbolic Execution for Finding Bugs’’]{.mycredit}

### example 1 in angr  {.smaller}


```
p = angr.Project("./example1", load_options={'auto_load_libs': False})

foo_addr = p.loader.main_object.get_symbol('foo').rebased_addr
INTERESTING_addr = p.loader.main_object.get_symbol('INTERESTING').rebased_addr
input_a = claripy.BVS('initial_a', 32)
input_b = claripy.BVS('initial_b', 32)
init_state = p.factory.call_state(foo_addr, input_a, input_b)

simgr = p.factory.simulation_manager(init_state)
print("at beginning:", simgr)
simgr.explore(find=INTERESTING_addr)
print("after explore:", simgr)
for state in simgr.found:
    found_a = state.solver.eval(input_a)
    found_b = state.solver.eval(input_b)
    print(f'(a, b) = ({found_a}, {found_b})')

```
 <hr />
 
```
after explore: <SimulationManager with 4 deadended, 4 found>                                             
(a, b) = (0, 1)                                                                                          
(a, b) = (0, 5)                                                                                          
(a, b) = (1, 2)                                                                                          
(a, b) = (9, 2147483648)   

```
